LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEC-20-2021 HEARING HELD RE: PLAINTIFF DOORDASH, INC.'S PETITION AND PETITION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AND FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) COUNSEL FOR THE PETITIONER IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: ETHAN P. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-07-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Petition To Enforce Settlement And For Injunctive Relief
Off Calendar Dec-20-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. MEL M.C. COLE, Esq. of Littler Mendelson P.C. (Tel: 415-433-1940) for petitioner DoorDash, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DOORDASH, INC.'s Petition And Petition To Enforce Settlement And For Injunctive Relief.

Matter discussed re: petitioner's counsel's procedural question.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner DoorDash, Inc.'s petition to enforce settlement agreement and for injunctive relief is denied without prejudice. Petitioner was unable to serve Respondent Adnan Mousa with the moving papers. (Cole Reply Decl. par. 4.) Mr. Mousa is a party to the settlement agreement Petitioner seeks to enforce, and therefore is a necessary party to the instant motion.

The Court notes that although Petitioner timely filed and served its petition on the DLSE, the DLSE did not file any opposition to the petition. The Court also notes that Petitioner paid the agreed upon settlement payment of $1,300 one business day after the Friday, October 8, 2021 deadline, which arguably constituted substantial performance with the settlement agreement. (See Magic Carpet Ride LLC v. Rugger Investment Group L.L.C. (2019) 41 Cal.App.5th 357, 364 [doctrine of substantial performance applies when a party performs but misses a deadline; where time is not of the essence of a contract, payment made within a reasonable time after the due date stated in the contract constitutes compliance therewith].) However, the Court will not decide that issue in Mr. Mousa's absence.

Counsel for the petitioner is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com (302/EPS)
